commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates shared command-catalog presentation helpers, the WinUI chat composer slash popup, and shared tests to render grouped Session/Model/Tools/Agents slash-command rows with styling and auto-scroll.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows bucket-ordered flattening can put an earlier display bucket ahead of the global top search result. I did not run the Windows UI, but the ranking and selection paths are clear from the diff, current main, and the inspected screenshot.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair is plausible because the blocker is a concrete selection-order bug in the slash palette plus missing shared presentation coverage.

target.title: Chat: group the / command palette by buckets with styled rows
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem:** The Windows chat `/` slash palette (added in #890) rendered a flat, alphabetically-ordered command list capped at 8 items, so late-alphabet run options (`/verbose`, `/reasoning`, `/trace`, `/usage`) were truncated off and there were no category subheadings — diverging from Mac's grouped menu. - **Why it matters:** Mac parity for discovering session/run controls via `/`. The run options were effectively hard to reach from the palette. - **What changed:** Group the `/` palette by Mac's **Session / Model / Tools / Agents** buckets with uppercase subheadings and Mac-style rows (icon · `/name` · `[args]` · right-aligned description · accent-tinted `N options` badge). The list filters/ranks live as you type, and the keyboard-selected row auto-scrolls into view. - **User impact:** Typing `/` shows a grouped, scrollable, Mac-faithful command menu; run options appear under **MODEL**; the list narrows/ranks as you type with the best match kept as the default Enter target. - **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No custom "Run options" (•••) menu (Mac has none) and no `sessions.patch` UI plumbing. The gateway command catalog, the arg-choice picker, and the Enter/Tab/Escape/loading behavior from #890 are unchanged. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor - [ ] Docs / instructions - [x] Tests / validation - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore / infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Tray / WinUI UX - [ ] Windows node capability - [ ] Local MCP / `winnode` - [ ] Gateway / connection / pairing - [ ] Setup / onboarding - [ ] Permissions / privacy / security - [x] Tests / CI / docs ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related #890 (inline `/`-triggered gateway command catalog menu) - [ ] Related to a bug or regression ## Validation After code changes, on Windows ARM64 (`OPENCLAW_REPO_ROOT` set to the worktree): - `./build.ps1` — ✅ all 5 projects built (Shared, Cli, WinNodeCli, SetupEngine, WinUI) - `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — ✅ **Passed: 2680, Failed: 0, Skipped: 31** - `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — ✅ **Passed: 1443, Failed: 0** New focused tests in `CommandCatalogPresentationTests.cs` cover the Mac 4-bucket mapping (`Bucket`), bucket ordering (`GroupBy` with `DisplayOrder`), labels, and within-group relevance preservation. ## Real behavior proof - **Environment tested:** Windows ARM64, non-isolated local run (`./run-app-local.ps1 -NoBuild -AllowNonMain`), connected to a gateway exposing the `commands.list` catalog. - **PR head / commit tested:** `6d9a4720` - **Exact steps or command run:** 1. Open Chat, type `/` — observe the grouped palette. 2. Arrow-navigate (↑/↓) through the groups past the visible fold. 3. Type `/reas` — observe live filtering down to `/reasoning` under **MODEL**. - **Evidence after fix:** see screenshots below. - **Observed result:** Palette renders uppercase **SESSION / MODEL / TOOLS / AGENTS** subheadings; run options (`/verbose`, `/reasoning`, `/exec`, `/think`, …) appear under **MODEL**; each row shows `icon · /name · [args] · right-aligned description · "N options"` badge; the list filters and re-ranks as you type; the selected row scrolls into view during arrow navigation. - **Screenshot/artifact links verified?** `Yes` - **Not verified / blocked:** N/A > Rubber-duck review: dual review (GPT-5.5 + Claude Opus 4.8) — no blocking issues. Consensus should-fixes addressed: removed the item cap so no bucket is silently dropped (also removed the `HashSet` re-trim), preserved relevance order within groups (best match = default Enter target), `NormalizeKey` prefers the text-surface `Name`, and defensive accent-brush cast. Re-validated green after fixes. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` (this is presentation/ordering only over the existing `commands.list` catalog; no new command is invoked) - Data access scope changed? `No` ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
target.author: bkudiess

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
